titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of TNA DL 25/89

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=856 status=live modified=2021-04-04 16:31:01.382225+00 | translation moa=857 status=live modified=2021-04-04 16:31:03.0824+00

p.cl: Omnibus hoc scriptum visuris uel audituris Angnes Auenel filia primogenita quondam Rauf Auenel salutem In domino .

Noueritis me
concessisse . et per fustum et baculum resignasse et pro me et heredes meis . omnino quietum clamasse . domino Roberto de Brus . domino vallis Anand’
totam terram quam habui uel habere potero In tenemento de withstan . In feodo de Thongera[y]th . et Holgrilemire et totum pratum meum de
Capilker . cum omnibus aliis pertinentiis ad dictam terram ubique pertinentibus . uel aliquo modo pertinere valentibus sine aliquo retenemento  Habendum
et Tenendum dicto domino Roberto de Brus uel heredibus suis uel suis assignatis In perpetuum Ita quod nec ego uel heredes mei . nec aliquis nomine nostro
aliquid Iuris uel clamii In dicta terra . cum ius pertinenciis de certo exigere poterimus uel clamare .

In cuius rei testimonium huic scripto sigillum meum
apposui .

Hiis testibus . Dominis . Hunfr’. de Gardino . Roberto de Harryngtona . Dauid de Thorthorald’. Hunfr’ de Bosco militibus
Galfr’ de Caldecote . Ada flamank’. Roberto forestar’ . Iohanne de Resholes et aliis .
To all who shall view or hear this writing Agnes Avenel the first-born daughter of the late Ralph Avenel sends greeting in the Lord.

Know me to have granted, and by rod and staff to have resigned, and for myself and my heirs entirely quitclaimed, to the lord Robert de Brus the lord of Annandale the entire land which I had or shall be able to have in the holding of 'Withstan' in the feu of Tundergarth and 'Holgrilmire' and my entire meadow of Capilker, with all other pertinents belonging to the said land everywhere, or by any means being able to belong, without any withholding, for the said lord Robert de Brus or his heirs or his assigns to have and to hold for ever so that neither I or my heirs, nor anyone in our name shall certainly be able to exact or claim any right or claim in the said land.
In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to this writing.

With these witnesses, the lords Humphrey de Jardin, Robert of Harrington, David of Torthorwald, Humphrey del Bois, knights, Geoffrey of Caldicote, Adam Fleming, Robert the forester, John of Resholes, and others.
